sections.description: Suite orientation: what Charlotte is (a single-domain crawler that maps a site, records but never follows first-tier external links, verifies links — including inside PDF/Office docs — and writes a self-contained HTML report) plus the pick-your-tool table linking each entry point.

The flagship Node crawler — the primary, recommended tool, kept whole as one coherent reference with its own internal ### TOC (~770 lines). Covers requirements, quick start, common runs, the full option list, multi-site runs, scope/depth, rate limiting + robots/adaptive-backoff/auto-resume, document-link scanning, the --recheck-from / --rebuild-from modes, the headless Playwright second opinion (crawl-render.js), large-crawl checkpoints + resume (--state/--resume), mid-crawl re-tuning (--tune-file), memory tuning, the self-contained HTML report, and the allowlist.

The Windows GUI front-end (crawl-gui.hta) that builds the crawl.js command line and launches it: requirements, the optional crawl-gui-domains.txt (default start URLs) and crawl-gui-config.txt (default option values) files, and how to run it.
The in-browser crawler variant (web-crawler.html) that runs entirely in a tab — for a live, interactive report with no Node install.
The zero-dependency CORS proxy (local-cors-proxy.js) that lets web-crawler.html cross origins from a file:// page.
Politeness guidance: honest User-Agent/headers, rate limits, and respecting robots.txt — how to crawl responsibly.
